name: My Phoenix personal injury case—insurance denied citing my ChatGPT use, now what?
description: Get lawyer to appeal, emphasize real evidence over AI chats—they're just one piece.[1]

mainEntity.name: My Phoenix personal injury case—insurance denied citing my ChatGPT use, now what?

acceptedAnswer.text: Get lawyer to appeal, emphasize real evidence over AI chats—they're just one piece.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description:
Appeal denial with attorney, arguing AI irrelevance or context.[1] *Heppner*-like rulings make AI chats admissible for inconsistencies, but not sole denial basis.[1][2] Arizona follows discovery norms.

**Key factors:**
- Prove chats don't admit fault.
- Strong medical/witness evidence overrides.
- Bad faith if denial pretextual.

Demand policy copy, file suit if bad faith; counter with non-AI proof. Avoid adjuster solo talks.[1] Escalate to lawyer immediately.
